content.full_text:
June 11, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

During the most recent election I came across several endorsement mailers which indicated that the candidate (or proposition) designated by an "*" paid for and authorized their appearances in the mailer.

Please inform me if there are any civil, administrative, or criminal penalties involved in the event a candidate (or a proposition) so designated by an "*" did not actually authorize such an appearance or if a candidate (or proposition) did not pay for such an appearance, or both.

Also, please inform me if the rules regarding same apply equally to a PAC sponsoring such a mailer; to a qualified political party sponsoring such a mailer; and to a "slate mailer" organization.

I am not in a great hurry to receive answers to the questions posed above but I believe I will probably need the answers to them by the first week in October 1990 (for possible use in the November 1990 election).

Sincerely,

John M. Hanley

505 W. Olive Avenue, #115
Sunnyvale, California 94086
